title: Stock picks of the week: 4 stocks with consistent score improvement and upside potential of up to 28%
summary: There are two factors that the Indian markets will have to deal with in the coming days. The first, of course, is the reignited US-Iran war (or failed ceasefire). If it continues, and oil inches higher and remains elevated, things might change. Then there is the second factor. Our selected stocks for today depict a strong upward trajectory in their overall average score which is based on five key pillars: Earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum. This implies that there has been a significant improvement in their market outlook in the given time frame.

title: Bank holiday today? Check if SBI, HDFC, ICICI are open or closed on 18 July— RBI calendar here
summary: Most public and private banks across India will remain open today, on Saturday, 18 July. According to the Reserve Bank of India (RBI)'s holiday calendar, bank operations will continue as normal since it is the third Saturday of the month

title: BSE, Sona BLW among 10 midcap stocks that rose up to 53% in 2026. Do you own any?
summary: Midcap stocks have staged a strong comeback in 2026, with Oracle Financial Services, Solar Industries and BSE delivering gains of up to 53% as market sentiment and earnings outlook improved.

title: NIfty IT logs best weekly gains since Oct 2025
summary: Indian IT stocks experienced a significant rebound this week.  The Nifty IT index saw its best weekly gain since October 2025.  This rally followed a sell-off in Asian AI and semiconductor stocks.  First-quarter earnings from major IT firms showed minimal AI disruption.  Investor confidence improved, leading to some short-covering in the sector.

title: D-Street bucks Asian markets' meltdown
summary: The NSE’s Nifty 50 rose 261.55 points, or 1.1%, to close at 24,334.3, while the BSE Sensex gained 964.58 points, or 1.25%, to end at 78,151.45.

title: Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 20 July 2026
summary: Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking, highlighted that from a technical perspective, the index is now approaching the crucial resistance zone near 200-Day EMA, where a decisive breakout could trigger further upside momentum.

title: Stocks to buy under  ₹200: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell
summary: Mehul Kothari from Anand Rathi suggests a cautious approach for traders, recommending a decisive close above 24,400 for a bullish outlook. For stocks under  ₹200, he recommends Suzlon Energy, IDBI, and Zee Entertainment with specified buy prices and targets.

title: Axis Bank Q1 preview: Profit, NII to stay healthy; NIM likely to shrink due to deposit repricing, competitive funding
summary: As Axis Bank gears up to announce its Q1 results, analysts predict significant growth in net interest income and net profit. However, a potential decline in net interest margin due to competitive pressures and deposit repricing raises concerns.

title: ICICI Bank Q1 results preview: Net profit seen up 3.1% YoY to  ₹13,164 crore; NIMs, asset quality may remain stable
summary: ICICI Bank Q1 results preview: ICICI Bank’s loan growth in the June quarter is expected to be healthy at 18.5% YoY, led by gold loans, corporate loans, personal loans and mortgages. Deposits are seen rising 15.2% YoY.

title: HDFC Bank Q1 results preview: Net profit likely to rise 6% YoY on strong loan growth, NIMs to remain flat
summary: HDFC Bank Q1 results preview: HDFC Bank Q1 results are expected to be mixed, with NIM likely to remain largely stable sequentially, while asset quality is expected to remain steady. Analysts anticipate healthy double-digit loan growth, supported by strong business momentum.

title: Q1 results 2026: HDFC Bank to ICICI Bank, Axis Bank among over 20 companies to declare Q1 earnings today; full list here
summary: On July 18, over 20 companies, primarily banks, will announce their Q1 FY27 earnings, providing crucial insights into India's banking sector. Key indicators include profit growth and asset quality, with strong focus on management commentary amid rising geopolitical tensions.

title: The 10-10-10 SIP rule: A simple formula for long-term wealth creation
summary: The 10-10-10 SIP rule helps investors build long-term wealth by investing for 10 years, assuming 10% annual returns and increasing SIP contributions by 10% each year. Learn how step-up SIPs can create a larger corpus through disciplined investing and compounding.
